[2018-03-06 23:22] wilson0862 (Wilson): "4) Being helpful is good, but it's also good to let people learn the game on their own, since the true joy of discovery is often maximized when you're doing the discovering on your own. If you're watching a new streamer try the game, don't backseat game or give them unsolicited advice. Most streamers don't like hints or spoilers unless they ask."
[2018-03-06 23:22] wilson0862 (Wilson): Such a huge fan of this inclusion in Elyot's announcement thing.
[2018-03-06 23:22] 1000mh (Henry): But will we listen.. Hmm
[2018-03-06 23:22] wilson0862 (Wilson): The playerbase is so damn impatient and backseat games the shit out of every new streamer, lmao
[2018-03-06 23:23] wilson0862 (Wilson): I don't think that's unique to us but I hate it so much, haha
[2018-03-06 23:23] http404error: I'll reiterate again that I love the process of discovering and learning Prismata so much, and getting to see that in new players is wonderful
[2018-03-06 23:23] http404error: Even if it's a little tilting
[2018-03-06 23:23] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yeah, I agree it's frustrating but that hurts me, not them.
[2018-03-06 23:23] http404error: I'll never get to learn the game again, which is a little sad!
[2018-03-06 23:23] http404error: Getting better is an adventure in itself but it doesn't have, like, really obvious gains.
[2018-03-06 23:23] http404error: It's 1 gold here, one damage there now.
[2018-03-06 23:24] mrguy888: I like to give info that they can understand and work with to figure stuff out by themselves but most people give advice that is a lot more confusing than helpful
[2018-03-06 23:24] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Tell them DD, DD, DDA, TTD, RRD.
[2018-03-06 23:24] wilson0862 (Wilson): Yeah, people will throw like advanced strats and it's like......... they don't even know how to absorb 2 on wall yet, relax.
[2018-03-06 23:25] http404error: I think if streamers just play the campaign they'll be adequately prepared to avoid most of the main complaints
[2018-03-06 23:25] http404error: Beyond "how the mechanics work", the most important macro knowledge points are things like "attackers provide valuable pressure, so time them carefully"
[2018-03-06 23:25] http404error: But like, how do you tell a noob that?
[2018-03-06 23:26] http404error: You don't really, they'll learn best from being shown up by Tarsier spam
[2018-03-06 23:26] 1000mh (Henry): Have any of you watched the Let's Play that Elyot posted a little while ago?
[2018-03-06 23:26] 1000mh (Henry): Their first game took over an hour x.x
[2018-03-06 23:27] mrguy888: I watched like 20 minutes until I knew top would win and then skipped to the end.
[2018-03-06 23:28] mrguy888: I would say they did really well for that being their first game with no other exposure.
[2018-03-06 23:28] 1000mh (Henry): P2 even took natural conduit if I remember :O
[2018-03-06 23:29] mrguy888: I strongly prefer people that spend 2 hours on their first game than people who spend 2 minutes and leave not knowing any more than when they walked in.

[2018-03-06 23:43] wilson0862 (Wilson): I believe that was one of the decisions to make it not f2p to start.
[2018-03-06 23:43] http404error: @StrShPlyr Try this: http://blog.prismata.net/2014/06/17/the-solution-to-frustrating-time-controls/
The Solution To Frustrating Time Controls - Prismata Blog
Many turn-based games have different systems for allocating time to players.  Sometimes, these systems result in very negative experiences.  Looking at Hearthstone, Magic Online, Poker, Chess, and Prismata, we analyze the effectiveness of these games at producing positive experiences through their time control systems. ____________________________________________________________________________________________________________________ In Hearthstone, there is a 90 second increment, with no …
[2018-03-06 23:45] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): @StrShPlyr I wouldn't ping people two messages in a row
[2018-03-06 23:46] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Also we have been asked numerous times to keep #ask_a_dev clean for visibility. Generally discussions arising from questions should be taken to another channel
[2018-03-06 23:46] http404error: @StrShPlyr You don't have anything to back that up, and you're wrong. Games don't run for 60 turns.
[2018-03-06 23:46] wilson0862 (Wilson): Thanks for being awesome, SpiritFryer. ❤

[2018-03-06 23:47] http404error: They were also playing highly inefficiently due to being new

[2018-03-06 23:48] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I feel partially responsible for the CEO fiends Anon :Lul:
[2018-03-06 23:48] mqp: you can think that time controls should be shorter or longer but you certainly found a shitty piece of evidence for your think

[2018-03-06 23:52] wilson0862 (Wilson): rip vacation time BibleThump
[2018-03-06 23:52] http404error: Well, Loonark actually does read feedback, I assume, but it lets them filter out the noise.
[2018-03-06 23:52] http404error: Yes, you can see that the lower part of the box is actually the flaps that would make up the bottom
[2018-03-06 23:54] saracen26: I need to put up one of those at work
[2018-03-06 23:54] elyot: @Saracen26 Anyone can leave Steam reviews but only people who bought the game on Steam actually can leave reviews that "count" in terms of the % positive negative and the Steam algorithm's rating of the game
[2018-03-06 23:54] elyot: Keys that we give away for free don't influence the score (which is good because we can be liberal with giving them away)
[2018-03-06 23:55] elyot: We only get reviews from people who actually looked at the game, scrutinized the steam page, and decided it was for them (and paid $25)
[2018-03-06 23:55] elyot: Unfortunately Kickstarter folks get fucked out of being able to leave a legitimate Steam review unless they purchase the game again
[2018-03-06 23:56] elyot: But nothing we can really do about that
[2018-03-06 23:56] http404error: Hell, I'll buy it again, it's a damn great game
[2018-03-06 23:56] saracen26: Ah that's good then... Might still get a few because Steam refunds being a thing. But it's not going to be epidemic.. should be restricted to just a few individuals.. Same happens with every game regardless of type, genre etc.
[2018-03-06 23:56] http404error: I never did redeem my Steam key yet so I can do that
[2018-03-06 23:59] iminabearsuit: Does it work, if we purchase the game as a gift?
[2018-03-07 00:00] saracen26: yeah should do  because it's still a steam purchase.
[2018-03-07 00:00] http404error: Well, the gift recipient is the one who can leave a review, not you
[2018-03-07 00:00] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): So, on average, turns last what, 20s?
[2018-03-07 00:01] http404error: @StrShPlyr On average, you use up all your time, so probably ~95% of the time control per turn
[2018-03-07 00:01] http404error: Early turns often have high confidence so those go into the time bank at savings rate
[2018-03-07 00:02] http404error: Past the first few though, you'll be running the clock down most games
[2018-03-07 00:03] http404error: Game length is highly dependent on the builds though, just like StarCraft
[2018-03-07 00:03] http404error: Which you would know if you played the game
[2018-03-07 00:03] http404error: You might also know that there are a ton of time control settings
[2018-03-07 00:03] http404error: And you can choose what to queue up with
[2018-03-07 00:03] saracen26: Unless you enter a Blitz event.. then 6 seconds, because why not 😛
[2018-03-07 00:04] http404error: I never really appreciated that Prismata is a glaring exception to "always play on Fastest" until Elyot pointed it out
[2018-03-07 00:04] http404error: I'm highly amused by that
[2018-03-07 00:05] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): What's the most interesting setup.
[2018-03-07 00:05] http404error: Prismata also has super low downtime compared to other strategy games because you are incentivized to pass quickly on your early turns.
[2018-03-07 00:06] saracen26: It's an incredible way to play is Blitz.. 1st time I saw it yesterday... game speed is incredible and certainly one for the vets, I doubt I'd ever get good enough to hammer out hotkeys that fast and be able to think... Closing in on my 40's my brain isn't what it used to be.
[2018-03-07 00:06] http404error: @StrShPlyr They're all interesting, all %BIGNUMBER% of them

[2018-03-07 00:24] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): Do DD, DD, DDA, TTD, RRD.
[2018-03-07 00:25] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): After just follow with Random set.
[2018-03-07 00:28] http404error: Hey, that's a semi-decent opening
[2018-03-07 00:28] iminabearsuit: Until you play against anyone above tier 8.
[2018-03-07 00:28] http404error: Well hey, that's not too bad for someone who's never played a single game
[2018-03-07 00:28] http404error: The game does teach you that one, just so you know
[2018-03-07 00:29] iminabearsuit: I mean, he's read a lot of the wiki. So he could just copy paste any of those as well.
[2018-03-07 00:29] http404error: Oh right, the wiki..
[2018-03-07 00:29] http404error: Someone should update that
[2018-03-07 00:29] strshplyr5046 (StrShPlyr): No, was just checking.
[2018-03-07 00:29] http404error: Here's an interesting topic - I have a pretty smart friend I tried to get into the game, but he had a pretty singular complaint about why he didn't like it.
[2018-03-07 00:30] http404error: In a lot of sets, many units/tech choices are traps
[2018-03-07 00:30] http404error: So you can't always build what you want
[2018-03-07 00:30] http404error: And you can't be "a killer green player" or whatever
[2018-03-07 00:30] http404error: Just because green might suck in a given set
[2018-03-07 00:30] http404error: Mirrors do happen a lot in Prismata
[2018-03-07 00:30] http404error: I'm wondering what y'all think about that
[2018-03-07 00:31] http404error: I think Tech is really cool in Prismata, and the way countering your opponent works is also cool, but I wonder if the effect of countering might be a bit weak.
[2018-03-07 00:31] http404error: I think Chill is one of the strongest mechanics in the game's design because of how much it pushes hard-counter dynamics
[2018-03-07 00:31] mtanzer: the effect of countering is strong but good players know what counters what, that's what leads to the mirrors
[2018-03-07 00:32] wilson0862 (Wilson): Symmetrical games are definitely way more common than asymmetrical ones but the latter do exist.
[2018-03-07 00:32] Deleted User (Deleted User): >Prismata also has super low downtime compared to other strategy games because you are incentivized to pass quickly on your early turns.

Are you though? my 30s opponents didn't get the memo!
[2018-03-07 00:33] mtanzer: I'd say the opposite, that the best pris players spend a lot of time on the early turns thinking
[2018-03-07 00:33] wilson0862 (Wilson): You're just biased because you take forever.
[2018-03-07 00:33] mtanzer: hey I know I'm slow but I'm not the only one!
[2018-03-07 00:33] wilson0862 (Wilson): (I'm kidding. Don't hurt me.)
[2018-03-07 00:34] http404error: I'm definitely impressed with how often asymmetric games come up given what I know about game design and the math, but my friend still had that complaint!

2) If something like 45 in tournaments is standard, then analysis and/or stalling abuse is (too) incentivised

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: playing fast times is a decent solution to 3 which cooledcannon does.

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: I think we need to give him some example turns and explain everything we think about

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: your second concern is pretty confusing to me because i value the aesthetics of playing and watching a high-quality, close to the best possible game a lot

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: but i don't really value a few minutes of my time over the course of the game nearly as much

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: actually watching streams might be the easiest way to get this information across

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: then he can see someone's thought process

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: so i don't sympathize with wanting to sacrifice the quality of play to prevent "stalling abuse"

messages.author: mrguy888
messages.content: Taking your time to play the best you can isn't abuse cooledcannon.

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I understand why a 2ker would take a long time, but if someone is making major mistakes but isn't new, I would actually advise them to drop 1-2 time settings

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: ok, i didn't understand that

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: they make the same mistakes, but don't overthink things

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: It still sounds like you think you can typically find the perfectly optimal play in 45s.

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: That seems a little bit hyperbolic.

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: oh I think I get it, you're saying people are spending their time thinking but that thinking isn't actually helping them make better decisions

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I guess it might only be a problem on my end- but is it not a problem if both players in a tournemnt use all the time available in every match?

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: I do think that happens sometimes but not all the time

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I guess yeah i'm exaggerating to make a point http

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: if the stakes are high enough, the chances of that happening are guaranteed I think

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: that's so not a problem for me that i would still have fun watching if every game went literally five times slower

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I don't like being forced to do it nor my opponent, but it feels like prisoners dilemma if I don't do it

messages.author: http404error
messages.content: I think it's normal to run down the clock each game. But it sounds like you just prefer lower time controls.

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: i agree that it would be cool to also have blitz tournaments

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: yeah, I think you're right http

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: so you think faster time controls are the only way to avoid people being incentivized to use analysis mode during big tournaments?

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: I suppose it might be too much to cater to a niche audience, but I think something like 20s tournaments are perfect

messages.author: Deleted User
messages.content: you don't feel rushed, but you don't feel compelled to abuse analysis

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: this conversation reminds me of the campaign

messages.author: mtanzer
messages.content: I feel like you're just 2-3 times faster than me lol.  I wouldn't feel rushed on 60s and would feel like I had enough time to abuse analysis on like 120s

messages.content: prismata is going to release and have this big singleplayer mode where a ton of effort was put into both A) a pulp sci-fi story and B) prismata puzzles and then A and B get tied around each other in a little knot so that they are both the same game

messages.author: mqp
messages.content: i feel bemused that games do this because i basically care 100% about B and 0% about A
